vulnerability.reference: Chemists trust RL-optimized NMR at face value, neglecting to validate with independent methods.
vulnerability.generated: Chemists trust automated NMR output without independent verification, assuming accuracy under real low-field spin relaxation conditions.

impact.reference: Misidentification of compounds causes retracted papers and wasted research resources.
impact.generated: Misquantified carbon-13 yields inaccurate compound identification, corrupting research data and compromising food and soil safety screening.

dose_response.reference: RL-trained pulse sequences mis-characterize spin relaxation, enabling systematic quantification errors, which when trusted without independent validation by chemists, leads to misidentified compounds causing retracted papers and wasted resources.
dose_response.generated: RL-trained pulse sequences from simulated spectra mis-characterizing spin relaxation under real low-field conditions, through chemists using automated NMR spectroscopy, trigger uncritical trust, leading to misquantified carbon-13 and inaccurate compound identification, corrupting research data and compromising food and soil safety screening.
